strategy: This is a damage-cap race. His Death Equalize caps ALL damage, starting at 500 (Round 1) and rising to 1,000 (R3), 2,000 (R5), 4,000 (R7). Both his hits and YOUR hits are capped.

Every round he interrupts with a Deer Knight that would deal 100,000 Moon, limited to the current cap. Early rounds you only take about 500, but by Round 7 you take up to 4,000 per round, so kill him before the cap climbs too high.
Do NOT change the global: replacing it makes him Tower Shield and lets the Deer Knight deal its full, uncapped damage. Leave the Equalize alone.
He resists Any 60% and has 40% pierce; use prisms and pierce, and stack blades so each capped hit lands for the full cap value.

Blade hard in the low-cap rounds, then land your hits on rounds 5-7 when the cap (2,000-4,000) lets your attacks connect for real damage, racing him down before the Deer Knight outpaces your healing. His Deathblade is Aegis-protected.
cheatNotes: Being late after Round 2 brings a 100,000 Moon Ship of Fools (capped by the current Equalize). Join early.
The Equalize global caps all damage until it is changed, and you must NOT change it. Everything, yours and his, is limited to the cap.
Round 1 sets the damage cap to just 500. Your hits are tiny here; use these rounds to blade, not to hit.
Changing the global makes him Tower Shield AND lets his Deer Knight hit for full uncapped damage. Never touch the Equalize.
Every round he interrupts with a Deer Knight, 100,000 Moon but capped. As the cap rises this becomes lethal, so win before Round 7-9.
Round 5 raises the cap to 2,000; your hits start to matter.
Round 7 raises the cap to 4,000. This is your burst window before the incoming Deer Knight outscales your heals.
